StyleGAN employs a non-linear mapping network f : Z → W (an 8-layer MLP) to transform z into an intermediate latent vector w ∈ W of size Rd.
The same latent vector w, upon passing through a common affine transformation layer A at each level of the generator network, generates the style y = (ys, yb) = A(w) for that level.
Adaptive instance normalisation (AdaIN) is controlled by the style y via modulating the feature-map xf as AdaIN(xf, y) = ysσ(xf) + yb after each conv3x3 block of G(·).
Stochasticity is injected by adding one-channel uncorrelated Gaussian noise image to each layer of the network before every AdaIN operation.

Due to the limited representability and disentanglement of a single latent vector w ∈ W, the input is embedded in the extended W + latent space consisting of different latent vectors w+ ∈ W + of size Rk×d, one for each level of the generator network G(·).

The concept of photo-to-photo mapping being an easier task than sketch-to-photo mapping motivates the introduction of a photo-to-photo mapper Er(·) as a teacher to guide the learning of the sketch-mapper Es(·), highlighting the relationship between the two mapping tasks.

The photo-to-photo mapper Er(·) is structurally identical to the baseline sketch-mapper Es(·) with the aim of reconstructing the input photo at the output, emphasizing the close relation between the two mappers and their functions.

The latent vectors predicted by Er, once trained, act as ground-truth additionally supervising Es via a distillation loss, indicating a direct relationship where Er guides and supervises the learning process of Es.

The conditional sketch-to-photo generation model addresses a key challenge related to existing models by considering input abstract sketches as a hard constraint, resulting in deformed output images.

The conditional sketch-to-photo generation model introduces a novel supervised sketch-to-photo generation model that explicitly handles sketch-photo locality bias, enabling it to generate photorealistic images even from highly abstract sketches.
The novel supervised sketch-to-photo generation model is based on an autoregressive latent mapper that maps a sketch to a pre-trained StyleGAN’s latent space to generate an output.
The autoregressive latent mapper maps a sketch to a pre-trained StyleGAN’s latent space to generate an output in the proposed model.
Experiments show that the novel supervised sketch-to-photo generation model outperforms existing state-of-the-arts in generating photorealistic images from highly abstract sketches.
